title: Incorrectly Managed Heap in Priority Queue Leads to Wrong Results

statement.markdown: Implement a priority queue data structure using a binary min-heap. The priority queue should support the following operations:
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a sequence of integers representing the elements to be inserted into the priority queue.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the final priority queue after all elements have been inserted.
statement.notes: The priority queue should be able to handle up to 10,000 elements. Ensure that the implementation passes all test cases.

examples.explanation: The final priority queue after inserting all elements.
The final priority queue after inserting all elements.

editorial.title: Priority Queue Implementation
editorial.intuition: To implement a priority queue using a binary min-heap, we need to maintain the heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to use a list and apply heapify whenever an element is added or removed. However, this would result in a time complexity of O(n log n) for each operation.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a binary heap data structure, which ensures the heap property is maintained after each insertion or removal.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would involve implementing the heap operations (insert, removeMin, removeMax) using a binary heap data structure.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the implementation can be proven by ensuring that the heap property is maintained after each operation.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the implementation should involve testing all possible edge cases, including insertion and removal of elements.
commonMistakes: Failing to maintain the heap property after insertion or removal

functionSignature.cpp: void insert(int x, vector<int>& heap); int removeMin(vector<int>& heap); int removeMax(vector<int>& heap);
functionSignature.java: void insert(int x, int[] heap); int removeMin(int[] heap); int removeMax(int[] heap);
